observedSectionWindows.note: Single-page form-classification note with a title/OCR mismatch that should remain review-only.
Clarification note under Standard Valuation Law.

boundaries: Keep planning focused on the AG 09 family only.
Do not widen into AG 10 or later guideline files.
Keep the AG 09 main title / OCR mismatch visible as a review-only caveat.
Keep each companion file review-only until a human confirms the indexing choice.
Keep the family source-bound and review-only.
exclusions: No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.
No later guideline files in the same wave.

reviewStandards.regulatoryRequirement: Treat explicit must, shall, or required language as the requirement; preserve the citation and source context.
reviewStandards.definitionOrTerminology: Treat immediate annuities, deferred annuities, structured settlements, impaired lives, substandard mortality tables, and the actuarial-function references as terminology content unless they impose a duty.
reviewStandards.formulaContext: Treat valuation or mortality-table references as context that needs source-bound preservation rather than promotion.
reviewStandards.prescribedAssumption: Treat explicit mortality-table instructions or required table-selection language as prescribed assumptions unless the text clearly says otherwise.
reviewStandards.crossReferenceMapping: Treat references to the Standard Valuation Law, nonforfeiture laws, structured settlements, or deferred payments as mapping notes unless the operational text is included.
reviewStandards.backgroundContent: Treat the title line and framing sentence as background content unless they impose a duty.
reviewStandards.boundaryControlWindow: Treat each small file window as a review-only window that should not be merged forward.
reviewStandards.requiresHumanInterpretation: Treat the indexing choice and the OCR / encoded text layer as requiring human actuarial interpretation unless the text is explicit.
reviewStandards.sourceQualityCaveat: Treat text-layer noise or title/OCR mismatch as review-only caveats and do not widen the batch because of them.

appReady: export is sanitized, stable, and versioned
ragReady: review status no longer requires human disposition

currentChecksNeeded: Plan file exists and parses as JSON.
The AG 09 plan markdown exists and carries the expected headings.
Every proposed batch is review-only by default.
The plan keeps the family to four small files only.
Each planned batch has a matching batch definition.
The AG 09 batch definition file stays synchronized with the plan.
futureChecksSuggested: Family-wave checks for future short guideline families.
OCR/title mismatch checks for the AG 09 main file.
No learner-facing, app-ready, or RAG-ready promotion checks.
Text-layer quality caveat checks once extraction starts.
scriptImplications: scripts/ag09-batch-definitions.mjs should stay synchronized with the planned batch-084 through batch-087 slices when extraction is authorized.
scripts/batch-definitions.mjs should import the AG 09 definition file so the shared runner can find the planned batch IDs.
scripts/validate-scaffold.mjs should verify that the AG 09 plan file exists, parses cleanly, and preserves the review-only posture.
The shared runner should continue to honor the same stop conditions used for the VM-20 batches.

notes: Controlled AG 09 family planning artifact. Planned batches 084 through 087 remain review-only by default and are not extraction runs.
